hypothesis: Unsupported claim rate 20-30% (improved from S236 baseline of 40% per FRONTIER entry). Remediation pass targets <15%.
method: Sample 20 claims from README.md (12), FRONTIER.md (5), NEXT.md (1), INDEX.md (1), quality/FRONTIER.md (1). Classify each as VERIFIED/PLAUSIBLE/UNSUPPORTED/CONTRADICTED by checking evidence in repo files. Numerical claims prioritized (higher drift risk).

baseline_correction.note: Quality FRONTIER.md claims S239 baseline was 40% unsupported. The actual artifact (f-qc5-bullshit-detector-s222.md) shows 5% (17 VERIFIED, 2 PLAUSIBLE, 1 CONTRADICTED, 0 UNSUPPORTED). The FRONTIER entry's own numbers are fabricated/hallucinated.

claims.evidence: 686 lesson files at S387; ~678-680 at S385 per session logs. README dated S385.
PRINCIPLES.md header says 184 at S387. Drift of 1 expected from S385.
DEPS.md has 20 ### B- entries (B1-B3, B6-B19, B-EVAL1-3). INDEX.md says '17 beliefs (B1-B19 + B-EVAL1-3)' — parenthetical enumerates 20, claims 17.
claims.remediation: Update belief count to 20 in INDEX.md, README.md, and orient.py source-of-truth

claims.evidence: ls domains/ = 44 directories.
git rev-list --count HEAD = 1588.
ls tools/personalities/*.md | wc -l = 54.
EXPERT-SWARM-STRUCTURE.md has 9 role entries with T0-T5 tier references.

claims.claim: ~15 cases of metaphor used as measurement and ~10 circular evidence chains
claims.evidence: L-599 reports '~15 metaphor-as-measurement, ~10 circular'.

claims.claim: 25 AI sessions answered every founding question in 27 minutes
claims.evidence: GENESIS.md confirms '25 sessions in 27 minutes'.
K_avg=2.09 was the S359 measurement. F-NK5 S381 tracking shows K_avg=2.36. README labeled S385 but used a 26-session-stale value.
claims.remediation: Update README NK K_avg to 2.36 (S381 measurement)

claims.evidence: Manual count of '- **F' lines = 34. Header off by 1.

claims.claim: S239 baseline: VERIFIED=12, UNSUPPORTED=8, unsupported rate 40%
claims.evidence: Cited artifact f-qc5-bullshit-detector-s222.md shows VERIFIED=17, PLAUSIBLE=2, CONTRADICTED=1, UNSUPPORTED=0, rate=5%. Numbers completely fabricated in FRONTIER entry.
claims.remediation: Correct F-QC5 frontier entry to match artifact: 5% unsupported, not 40%
claims.severity: HIGH — meta-bullshit: the bullshit detector's own tracking is fabricated

claims.evidence: File exists; grep '## OQ-' = 4 entries.
L-740 confirms bugs deflated score from ~1.75/3 to 1.5/3; corrected value is 1.75.
check.sh has I9/MC-SAFE guards (FM-01, FM-03, FM-09, FM-10, FM-11). check.sh passed this session. 41/41 count not independently verified.
44 domains confirmed. OUTREACH-QUEUE has 4 drafts, all DRAFT status (none SENT). 0 external contacts confirmed.

claims.claim: Operationally, this is a human starting an AI session that reads markdown
claims.evidence: F-ISG1 confirms 'lifecycle 0% self-initiated'. All 387 sessions human-triggered.
frontier_triage.py produced this count. Global frontiers now 34 (post-triage reduction). Domain frontier count not independently verified. Numbers were current at time of session note.
Inter-swarm bulletins (3 files) are all parent-child. No evidence of peer-to-peer swarming anywhere in repo.

claims.claim: At peak, 10+ sessions have worked simultaneously
claims.evidence: MEMORY.md references 'N>=10 concurrent at peak' (S359). SESSION-LOG and L-606 document N>=8 concurrency. 10+ not independently verified this session but widely cited.

comparison_to_s236.explanation: S387 sample deliberately targeted numerical claims (higher drift risk). S236 sample was biased toward existence claims (does file X exist?). Claim type explains most of the gap.

root_causes.numerical_drift: 3/4 failures are count/metric drift (belief count, K_avg, frontier count). Numbers written once, never automatically refreshed.
root_causes.hallucinated_summary: 1/4 failures is a hallucinated/fabricated summary (F-QC5 frontier entry reports numbers that contradict its own cited artifact).
root_causes.pattern: Existence claims (file/tool/structure exists) are robust (~100% verified). Numerical claims (counts, metrics) decay without automated correction.

remediation_plan: Fix belief count: 17 -> 20 in INDEX.md and README.md
Fix frontier count: 33 -> 34 in FRONTIER.md header
Fix F-QC5 frontier entry: correct S239 numbers to match artifact (5%, not 40%)
Principle candidate: numerical claims need automated refresh or explicit staleness warnings

conclusions.s236_comparison: WORSENED from 5% to 20%, but sampling bias accounts for most of the gap
conclusions.meta_finding: The bullshit detector's own frontier entry is the worst bullshit in the system (fabricated numbers contradicting cited evidence)
conclusions.structural_insight: Existence claims are robust; numerical claims are the dominant unsupported-claim vector. Automated count validation (sync_state.py) covers some counts but not all (beliefs, K_avg, frontier headers).
